danger_signals.visual_form: structures that are visually distinct from terrain; invulnerable to small arms fire
danger_signals.meaning: active threat source; requires explosive weapons to neutralize

danger_signals.visual_form: jeeps, tanks, helicopters rendered distinctly from infantry
danger_signals.meaning: escalated threat level; superior firepower compared to foot soldiers

danger_signals.visual_form: rivers (slow soldiers, disable ranged capability), quicksand, mines, booby traps visually indicated
danger_signals.meaning: environmental danger; movement restriction and death traps
ranged threat; cannot be defeated with standard weaponry

danger_signals.visual_form: enemy soldiers become visually more numerous and aggressive as game progresses
danger_signals.meaning: world-state progression toward difficulty; player's initial firepower advantage erodes

safety_signals.meaning: resource replenishment; grenades and rockets ammunition restoration

safety_signals.visual_form: ability to split unit into smaller visual groups
safety_signals.meaning: tactical positioning; reduced exposure for soldiers

safety_signals.visual_form: clear sightlines without hazards or obstacles
safety_signals.meaning: reduced immediate danger; allows formation and controlled advance

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: player-controlled soldiers visually distinct from enemy infantry through uniform/silhouette
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: squad grouping and cursor-based control UI indicates player faction

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: enemy infantry rendered differently from player troops; spawn from designated buildings
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: buildings and military installations indicate enemy presence and control

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: player troops visually outnumber enemy formations; enemy infantry rendered as less dense
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: enemies die quickly to single rounds; player firepower dominates initially

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: enemy infantry becomes more numerous; vehicles and turrets appear with greater frequency
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: player firepower advantage erodes; enemies become more powerful as progression continues

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: jungle, snow, desert, and other biome-specific terrain rendering; unique structures like igloos and snowmobiles
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: level-specific hazards manifest visually (rivers, quicksand, snow terrain); movement mechanics adapt to environment

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: ammunition scarcity communicated through supply crate distribution; limited explosive weapon availability
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: player must conserve resources and plan carefully; wasting weapons risks mission failure

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: enemy swarms rendered across screen; player squad appears small relative to opposition
world_state_to_visual.meaning: strategic necessity enforced visually; caution and positioning critical

environmental_storytelling.example: jungle, snow, and desert settings with unique features (igloos, snowmobiles, rural inspiration from Emmerdale Farm) communicate geographic and cultural context without exposition
rivers, quicksand, mines, and booby traps positioned to suggest inhabited/contested territory; visual placement tells story of conflict zones
concentration of enemy spawning buildings and installations visually indicates fortified enemy positions; sparse structures suggest captured/neutral areas
limited supply crate placement and ammunition conservation mechanic visually reinforces anti-war theme; scarcity of resources mirrors real military logistics

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: move at normal speed; maintain firing capability
movement slowed visibly; firing capability disabled; vulnerability increased
die to single machine gun rounds; spawn from buildings; visually numerous but individually weak
require multiple rounds to kill; spawn more frequently; visually more aggressive
invulnerable to standard weaponry; require specialized tactics; visually mobile and threatening
can be retrieved for ammunition or shot to destroy nearby enemies; placement suggests strategic choices

notes: Cannon Fodder employs darkly humorous visual communication with an underlying anti-war message. The game communicates world-state primarily through environmental variety (biomes, hazards), enemy density escalation, and resource scarcity mechanics. Factions are distinguished not through explicit color/symbol systems but through behavioral and visual manifestation—player troops are controlled via point-and-click UI while enemies spawn from buildings. The overhead camera perspective provides omniscient world-view crucial to strategy elements. Visual fragility of troops (single-round kills) and small squad size relative to enemy swarms reinforce anti-war commentary. Sensible Software's design philosophy prioritized 'instant' action over complex war-game mechanics, resulting in visual simplicity with tactical depth.
